question: At what angle is the arc length half the perimeter of the circle?

A cuboid with dimensions 20 cm, 12 cm, and 10 cm is cut into 8 identical pieces by 3 cuts. What will be the total surface area of all the pieces?
A rectangle with the largest area is cut out of a circle with radius 5 units. What is the area of the remaining portion?

Which of the following statements is INCORRECT about the circle?
options: The area of a sector = 1/2 (arc length × R)
The radius of an incircle of an equilateral triangle of side s is s/(2√3)
The length of an arc = central angle × π r²
Circles are congruent if they have same radii while similar circles may have different radii.
answer: The length of an arc = central angle × π r²
